data.title: India pushes to ease international payments through homegrown network to rival Visa, Mastercard
data.description:
India is asking the Financial Action Task Force (FATF), a global money-laundering watchdog, to reduce compliance requirements for cross-border payments made through its homegrown system, several sources familiar with the discussions said.

Launched in 2016, the Unified Payment Interface (UPI) accounted for 83% of India's digital payments volume in 2024, up from 34% in 2019, and dominates the domestic retail payments sector.

The government now wants to increase the use of its own payments network by Indians travelling abroad, which could potentially tap into a growing market, and make the global cross-border payments market more competitive.

But its expansion for cross-border payments has been hampered by compliance requirements on smaller payments, to which payments made via networks such as Visa , Mastercard , and SWIFT are not subject, the sources said.

Government officials raised the issue at an FATF conference in Mumbai this week, two of the sources said, declining to be named because they are not authorised to speak to media.

The FATF, India's central bank and the federal finance ministry did not immediately respond to emails seeking comment.

Visa and Mastercard did not respond to requests for comment.

A final decision would depend on achieving a consensus among FATF member countries after the public consultation period, a third source familiar with the discussions said.

A public consultation on the FATF's "travel rule", which requires financial institutions to collect, hold, and transmit information about the sender and receiver of cross-border payments, is open until April 18.

In their current form, global anti-money laundering rules tend to favour both card networks and payments facilitated by the SWIFT payment system, the three sources said.

Central Bank Governor Sanjay Malhotra told the FATF gathering that "it would be desirable to make the (FATF's) travel rule technology-neutral," without specific mention of UPI.

India has so far inked deals with seven nations, including France and Singapore, which allow merchants to accept payments via the UPI.

($1 = 85.6175 Indian rupees)

data.title: Trade tensions weigh on euro zone stocks, Ubisoft jumps
data.description:
Key points:
  • Ubisoft surges after Tencent investment
  • German consumer sentiment remains unchanged amid uncertainty
  • UK stocks outperform, trade-sensitive DAX falls 0.6%
  • STOXX 600 down 0.2%

Euro zone shares dipped on Friday, at the end of a week marred by concerns over the global economic fallout of an intensifying trade war waged by U.S. President Donald Trump.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index fell 0.2% by 0935 GMT, after two straight days of declines and on track to shed 0.6% this week. A gauge of euro zone blue-chip shares (.STOXXE) dropped by a steeper 0.5%.

Among regional markets, Germany's trade-sensitive DAX index dropped 0.6%, while UK's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 outperformed with its 0.1% gain.

Ubisoft jumped 8.9% as the French video game maker said it had set up a subsidiary in which China's Tencent will invest 1.16 billion euros ($1.25 billion).

Europe's benchmark index retreated to two-week lows on Thursday after Trump's sweeping 25% import tariffs on the car industry raised jitters ahead of an April 2 deadline on reciprocal tariffs on U.S. trading partners.

"There's the impact of (tariffs) on earnings of the EU market, but people also worry about knock-on impact of growth scare and higher inflation in the U.S.," said Roseanna Ivory, investment director at Aberdeen.

Investors are now awaiting the U.S. personal consumption expenditures data due later in the day to gauge the trajectory for further interest rate cuts.

Erratic U.S. trade policy has fuelled fears of higher prices and a hit to growth, souring investment sentiment towards the U.S.

Meanwhile, a rotation out of the U.S. stocks and expectations of a mega fiscal boost from Germany have helped spur the strongest start to the year in five years for the STOXX 600 index.

A survey showed German consumer sentiment is broadly unchanged heading into April, with a focus on saving, highlighting uncertainty among households.

Near-term catalysts that could drive European stocks are whether there is more stimulus in China focused on improving consumer sentiment, and progress in peace talks in the Ukraine-Russia war, Aberdeen's Ivory said.

Russian President Vladimir Putin suggested Ukraine be placed under a form of temporary administration to allow for new elections and the signature of key accords to reach a settlement in the war.

In other company news, Deutsche Bank fell 2.6% as the bank extended CEO Christian Sewing's contract, while its deputy and another top executive will depart as part of a management revamp, cementing the leadership team of Germany's largest lender for the next phase of its turnaround.

data.title: Singapore Shares End Lower After Notching Record Intraday HighMarket Talk
data.description: Singapore shares ended lower in a likely technical correction after rising for four consecutive sessions. The FTSE Straits Times Index declined 0.2% to 3972.43 after notching a record intraday high of 4005.18. Consumer and property stocks led the losses, with DFI Retail Group dropping 2.0% and Thai Beverage fell by 1.0%. CapitaLand Investment lost 1.1% and Hongkong Land was down 2.0%. Singapore Exchange was the best performer in this session, rising 0.9%. Index heavyweight Singtel rose 0.9%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Burberry Seems to Be Pursuing Right Revamp Strategy Despite DifficultiesMarket Talk
data.description: Burberry is early in its turnaround, but it seems to be pursuing the right strategy, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a note. The company, which is trying to revamp its brand, is expected to report a 8% decline in retail sales on a like-for-like basis for its fiscal 4Q. The analysts anticipate sequentially softer trends in the Americas due to political uncertainty in the U.S., which is consistent with the picture in the wider luxury sector. The guidance for fiscal year 2026 might disappoint the market initially, they say. The analysts expect the U.K. luxury group to guide for flat-low single-digit organic revenue growth and EBIT between 100 million and 150 million pounds. Shares are down 0.5% at 8.21 pounds. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Italian Consumer Morale Weakens to Over 2-Year Low
data.description:
The Italian consumer confidence index declined to 95 in March 2025, compared to market estimates and February’s 98.8, marking the weakest reading since November 2023.

A significant deterioration in both the economic and future outlook is evident, with the economic climate index falling from 100.2 to 93.2 and the future climate index declining from 96.6 to 91.1.

Meanwhile, the personal and current climate indices saw a more moderate decrease, dropping from 98.3 to 95.7 and from 100.5 to 97.9, respectively.

data.title: Correction to Myanmar Quake Article
data.description:
By Feliz Solomon

A series of powerful earthquakes struck central Myanmar on Friday, rocking the war-torn country's second-largest city and shaking skyscrapers in the capital of neighboring Thailand.

The epicenter of a 7.7-magnitude quake struck just east of Mandalay at a depth of 10 kilometers early afternoon local time, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. The initial temblor was followed by several smaller quakes, the USGS said.

Authorities in Myanmar didn't immediately comment on the damage, but there were reports of streams of wounded seeking treatment at a hospital in the capital, Naypyitaw.

Videos from Myanmar shared on social media showed residential buildings reduced to rubble and a bridge that had collapsed into a river.

The tremors were felt as far away as the Thai capital of Bangkok, where thousands of panicked residents streamed into the streets.

With a population of about 1.2 million, Mandalay is one of the most populous cities in Myanmar. The Southeast Asian nation has been ruled by a military junta since a 2021 coup, which set off years of civil conflict.

Myanmar is one of the poorest countries in Asia and home to some 55 million people. It has experienced devastating natural disasters, including earthquakes and cyclones.

Write to Feliz Solomon at feliz.solomon@wsj.com

data.title: Kia to produce hybrid cars at Hyundai's Georgia factory from next year
data.description:
South Korea's Kia Corp plans to produce hybrid vehicles at its affiliate Hyundai Motor's new U.S. factory starting in mid-2026 as it seeks to meet growing demand, Kia's chief executive said without disclosing the models.

Hyundai - the world's No. 3 automaker when including Kia Motors, in which it owns a 35% stake - broke ground for an all-electric car factory in the state of Georgia in 2022 under former President Joe Biden's administration, which promoted EV-friendly policies.

But in a shift in strategy, Hyundai now plans to add hybrid cars to the factory's production lines at a time when demand for pure electric cars is cooling and President Donald Trump's administration is threatening to end EV subsidies.

Kia vehicles will represent 40% of the Georgia facility's total production, Kia CEO Song Ho-sung told reporters on the sidelines of an opening ceremony for the factory on Wednesday.

Hyundai said it plans to expand the factory's annual production capacity to 500,000 vehicles from an originally planned 300,000, bringing Hyundai and Kia's combined total U.S. production capacity to 1.2 million vehicles per year.

The upgraded plan represents part of the $21 billion in investments announced by Trump and Hyundai chairman Euisun Chung at the White House earlier this week.

Trump announced 25% import tariffs on cars and auto parts on Wednesday, a move that may hurt companies, especially Asian automakers, which are among the largest vehicle exporters to the U.S.

After Mexico, South Korea is the world's largest exporter of vehicles to the United States, followed by Japan, according to data from S&P.

Kia Motors currently produces most of its hybrid cars, including its Sportage hybrid crossover, in South Korea.

Song said the new U.S. factory would help achieve Kia's target of boosting U.S. sales to 1.25 million vehicles a year, up from 850,000 currently.

data.title: RCS - Valeura Energy Inc. - Comment on Earthquake
data.description:
RNS Number : 6858C Valeura Energy Inc. 28 March 2025  

Comment on Earthquake

Singapore, March 28, 2025: Valeura Energy Inc. (TSX:VLE, OTCQX:VLERF) ("Valeura" or the "Company") reports that all of its personnel are accounted for and safe following the recent earthquake in neighbouring Myanmar.

At approximately 13:30 local time on Friday March 28, 2025, a strong earthquake struck central Myanmar, approximately 1,000 km from Bangkok Thailand. While certain buildings in Thailand were damaged, Valeura has confirmed that all of its facilities in the offshore Gulf of Thailand remain operating safely, with no immediate indications of damage.

data.title: Bulgaria's industrial PPI leaps 16% y/y in Feb
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), March 28 (SeeNews) - Bulgaria's total industrial producer price index (PPI) increased by an annual 16.2% in February as all observed sectors recorded rises, the statistical office said on Friday.

In comparison, industrial PPI in the country went up 12.5% on the year in January.

In February, producer prices soared by 42.6% year-on-year in electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply, by 30% in the mining and quarrying industry and by 11.4% in the manufacturing industry, the National Statistical Institute (NSI) said in a monthly report.

In monthly terms, the industrial PPI climbed by 2.8% in February on the back of a 6.5% price increase in mining and quarrying, as well as a 4.2% uptick in manufacturing prices. Meanwhile, prices in electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply fell by 1.2% compared to the prior month.

Producer prices on the domestic market added 19.1% year-on-year in the review month after growing 14.4% in January. This was mostly due to a 49.1% surge in electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply prices. Producer prices on the non-domestic market advanced by 12% on the year in February.

On an annual comparison basis, the most significant price growth in Bulgaria's manufacturing industry was registered in the manufacture of basic metals - at 36.2%. Domestic producer prices in the manufacture of food products went up by a yearly 30.5%.

Bulgarian producer prices, breakdown by industry (pct change):

Feb y/yFeb m/mJan y/yJan m/m
Industrial PPI, total16.22.812.54.9
-manufacturing11.44.28.56.1
-mining30.06.527.13.5
-utilities42.6-1.234.211.7

Source: NSI

data.title: France and Spain inflation data, ECB survey bolster case for rate cuts
data.description:
Inflation in March came in far below forecasts in two of the euro zone's largest economies, data showed on Friday, while consumer expectations for price growth remained muted, bolstering bets for another European Central Bank rate cut.

The ECB has cut interest rates six times since June and markets now see an 80% to 85% chance of another move in three weeks, as price pressures seem to be easing on more modest wage hikes and continued economic stagnation.

Inflation in France held steady at 0.9% in March and came in below expectations for 1.1%. In Spain the figure fell to 2.2% from 2.9%, well below forecasts for 2.6%.

These numbers indicate that the overall euro zone figure, due on April 1, could also come below expectations and inch closer to the ECB's 2% target sooner than some policymakers had forecast.

While a full-blown trade war with the United States could upset price expectations, the ECB doubled down on its view that the main impact would be on growth.

"The effect would be fundamentally on economic activity," ECB Vice President Luis de Guindos said. "For inflation, a trade war would have a negative effect, ultimately a tariff is a tax on imported goods, but over the medium term the reduction in economic activity would compensate this initial impact in some way."

Consumers also appeared relaxed by the price effect of geopolitical tensions with the ECB's own survey showing moderating inflation perceptions and steady price expectations.

Median expectations for inflation over the next 12 months held at 2.6% while for three years ahead it was steady at 2.4%, the ECB said.

"We have good news on inflation... the disinflation process is continuing," de Guindos said. "We are convinced that in the next few quarters we'll achieve our definition of price stability, which is 2%, on a sustainable basis."

Consumers remained more pessimistic about economic growth, however, anticipating a 1.2% contraction over the next 12 months after seeing a decline of 1.1% in January.

data.title: Höegh Autoliners ASA - Registration of share capital reduction
data.description:
Oslo, 28 March 2025: Reference is made to the stock exchange announcement madeby Höegh Autoliners ASA (the "Company") on 20 November 2024 regarding theCompany's extraordinary general meeting resolving to reduce the Company'ssharecapital by NOK 2 632 622 536.20, from NOK 2 823 392 285.20 to NOK 190 769 749, through a decrease of the nominalvalueof each share with NOK 13.80, from NOK 14.80 to NOK 1.

The resolution was announced by the Norwegian Register of Business Enterpriseson 25 November 2024, whereafter followed a six-week creditor notice period.Thecreditor period has been completed without objections.

The share capital reduction has now been registered with the NorwegianRegisterof Business Enterprises. After this, the Company's share capital is NOK 190769749, divided into 190 769 749 shares with a nominal value of NOK 1.

For further information, please contact:Andreas Enger, CEO andreas.enger@hoegh.com +47 901 31 228 Espen Stubberud, CFO espen.stubberud@hoegh.com +47 400 39 753

Investor Relationsir@hoegh.com

Media contact:Camilla Knappskog, Head of Communicationscamilla.knappskog@hoegh.com+47 926 66 156

About Höegh Autoliners: Höegh Autoliners is a leading global provider of RoRo (Roll On Roll Off)transportation services delivering cars, high and heavy and breakbulk cargoesacross the world. The Company operates around 40 RoRo vessels in global tradesystems and makes more than 2 000 port calls each year. Our purpose is todevelop innovative solutions for greener and more sustainable deep seatransportation. We are on a path to a zero emissions future and are workingclosely with customers and partners to achieve this. Höegh Autoliners has itshead office in Oslo, Norway and employs around 460 people in its 16 officesworldwide and around 1 200 seafarers.

data.title: High density polyethylene (HDPE) prices quote mixed in Europe
data.description: This week, HDPE prices displayed a mixed undertone in the European region.An industry source in Europe reported a Polymerupdate team member, "The European HDPE market experienced a week of stagnation, with discussions largely centered on tariff developments. The situation is complex, as materials exported from the United States could be impacted by tariffs imposed on Europe. Trading activity in the spot market was limited, driven by consistently low demand for spot volumes and increasing apprehensions regarding the potential effects of tariffs. Converters voiced uncertainty about US imports into Europe, with some market participants managing to obtain only technical grades of linear low-density polyethylene (LLDPE) and HDPE from US suppliers. Throughout the week, traders were cautious with their offers, with some even suspending US imports entirely until there is greater clarity regarding the tariff situation. Additionally, certain traders chose not to offer materials from shipments expected next month. The market remains constrained by the tariff issue, with participants citing the need to understand potential retaliatory measures by the end of the month. Prices have remained stable, with minimal trading activity observed.In spot markets, HDPE film grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1145-1155/mt FD North West Europe levels, a week on week decrease of Euro (-20/mt), while HDPE BM grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1145-1155/mt FD North West Europe levels, a fall of Euro (-10/mt) from last week. Meanwhile, HDPE injection grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1125-1135/mt FD North West Europe levels, stable from the previous week.In the contract markets, HDPE film grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1640-1645/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both gaining by Euro (+10/mt) from week on week. HDPE film grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1640-1645/mt FD NWE France levels, a rise of Euro (+10/mt) from last week. Meanwhile, HDPE film grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1370-1375/mt FD NWE UK levels, marginally up GBP (+5/mt) from the previous week.In the contract markets, HDPE BM grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1620-1625/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both rising by Euro (+10/mt) week on week. HDPE BM grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1620-1625/mt FD NWE France levels, up Euro (+10/mt) from the previous week. Meanwhile, HDPE BM grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1355-1360/mt FD NWE UK levels, a gain of GBP (+5/mt) from last week.In the contract markets, HDPE injection grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1580-1585/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both week on week higher by Euro (+10/mt). HDPE injection grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1580-1585/mt FD NWE France levels, up Euro (+10/mt) from the previous week. Meanwhile, HDPE injection grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1320-1330/mt FD NWE UK levels, a marginal rise of GBP (+5/mt) from last week.The source added, "In addition to tariff changes, the market's attention was directed towards the expected ethylene monomer contract price for April. The prevailing outlook suggested a decline from the March price, influenced by a reduced average naphtha price during the month. As April approached, market sentiment remained robust regarding contract prices, with most forecasts dependent on the resolution of the tariff issues. Some producers indicated strong order volumes for the month, whereas converters expressed caution, noting a lack of optimism for demand recovery in the construction and automotive industries. The level of uncertainty is significant. Ultimately, the situation hinges on the monomer price quotation and the evolving demand dynamics. FC Antwerp HDPE film prices were assessed at the Euro 1130-1170/mt levels, a week on week decline of Euro (-20/mt). FCA Antwerp HDPE BM prices were assessed at the Euro 1130-1160/mt levels, a fall of Euro (-10/mt) from last week. while FCA Antwerp HDPE injection prices were assessed at the Euro 1110-1140/mt levels, left unchanged from the previous week.Ethylene spot prices on Thursday were assessed at the Euro 845-855/mt FD North West Europe levels, marginally down Euro (-5/mt) from week on week. European Ethylene contract price for March 2025 settled at the Euro 1260/MT FD North West Europe levels. This price represents a marginal gain of Euro 2.50/MT from its February 2025 settlement levels.
